name: Santa Casilda Sanctuary and Santorcaz Loop
name_original: Santuario de Doña Casilda y San Torcaz — circular por la población de Buezo
description: Hike a moderate 7.2-mile circular route from Buezo, exploring the historic Sanctuary of Doña Casilda and panoramic views from San Torcaz.
region_intro: Hiking around Salinillas De Bureba offers diverse landscapes characterized by gentle hills, open fields, and traditional paths connecting to neighboring villages. The region features limestone rocks with an anticline fold, and trails often lead through oak groves. Notable natural attractions include the Cascadas de Salinillas de Bureba, such as Peña Borco and La Herradura, which are particularly impressive after rain. Best hiking trails around Salinillas De Bureba The most popular hiking route is Santa Casilda Sanctuary and Santorcaz Loop, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the historic Santa Casilda Sanctuary. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hostal Lumbreras – Partially Collapsed Building loop from Briviesca, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail offers views of the rural landscape near Briviesca, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Local hikers also love the Plaza Mayor de Briviesca – Street Life in Spain loop from Briviesca, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through Briviesca's urban and immediate surroundings, often completed in about 44 minutes. Hiking around Salinillas De Bureba is defined by gentle hills, open fields, and limestone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate routes. The routes in Salinillas De Bureba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 27 hikers have used komoot to explore Salinillas De Bureba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Salinillas de Bureba?
A: Hiking around Salinillas de Bureba offers diverse landscapes characterized by gentle hills, open fields, and traditional paths connecting to neighboring villages. The region features unique limestone rocks with an anticline fold, and many trails lead through scenic oak groves, providing panoramic views towards the Ebro valley and La Rioja.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant natural features to explore?
A: Yes, one of the most remarkable natural attractions is the Cascadas de Salinillas de Bureba, which includes waterfalls like Peña Borco and La Herradura (also known as Las Calderas). These are particularly impressive during periods of rain or snowmelt, offering a dynamic natural spectacle. You can also observe the 'pozo blanco' and 'pozo negro' near the Santa Casilda Sanctuary, historically believed to have medicinal properties.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, among the 9 available routes, there is at least one easy trail. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Briviesca – Street Life in Spain loop from Briviesca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.8-mile (2.9 km) path that takes about 44 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's surroundings.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit while hiking in the area?
A: The area around Salinillas de Bureba features several points of interest. A significant site is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Doña Casilda&lt;/a&gt;, a 16th-century Renaissance structure built on a limestone crag. This pilgrimage site also houses a Museum of Ex-votos. The village itself retains traditional Bureban architecture, adding to the authentic atmosphere.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the region are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042928560/santa-casilda-sanctuary-and-santorcaz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Casilda Sanctuary and Santorcaz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.2-mile (11.7 km) route that takes approximately 3 hours 27 minutes, exploring the area around the historic sanctuary.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Salinillas de Bureba?
A: The routes in Salinillas de Bureba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful, uncrowded nature of the area, which provides an ideal setting for tranquil walks and contemplation amidst diverse landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to reach the hiking areas by public transport?
A: While Salinillas de Bureba is a rural area, bus services connect it to larger towns. However, direct public transport access to specific trailheads might be limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Salinillas de Bureba or nearby villages, and plan for potential short walks or taxi services to reach your starting point.

Q: Are there places to park near the hiking trails?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In Salinillas de Bureba and surrounding villages, you can typically find parking in designated areas or near local amenities. For popular natural attractions like the Cascadas de Salinillas de Bureba, there are often informal parking spots available, though these can fill up during peak times.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Salinillas de Bureba?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. The waterfalls are most impressive after periods of rain or snowmelt, typically in late winter or early spring. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Most of the routes around Salinillas de Bureba are classified as moderate, offering a good challenge without extreme difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;FuenteDelPeregrino – IglesiaSantaMaría loop from Quintanavides&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) hike with significant elevation gain, taking around 2 hours 47 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Salinillas de Bureba region, especially on the traditional paths and rural routes. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access in specific natural parks or protected areas.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the hiking areas?
A: Salinillas de Bureba and its neighboring villages offer basic amenities. You can find local cafes and pubs in the villages for refreshments. For accommodation, there are often rural guesthouses or small hotels in the area, providing a comfortable base for exploring the trails. Larger towns like Briviesca, nearby, offer more extensive services.
tips: Due to popular beliefs, curative properties are attributed to the Pozo Negro de Santa Casilda. The area recovered by the Friends of Santa Casilda is the perfect place to rest for a while before going up to the Sanctuary.
Beautiful spring located under the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda, and next to a recreational area.
Small peak near the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda, with beautiful views and easy access.
Accessible from the south (Doña Casilda) or from the north (Piérnigas). The views are very nice.
This Sanctuary is located in a magnificent location, it is worth the site, the views, and the cave of Doña Casilda (in the back of the Monastery).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike from Buezo?
faqs.answer: The hike is estimated to take around 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 11.66 km (7.25 miles).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse terrain, including rocky ascents and descents, particularly around the Alto de la Varga and the sanctuary itself. There are also gentler paths through fields and forested areas with pine, holm oak, and gall oak. Some sections might involve walking near a stream, and you'll encounter varied surfaces throughout.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While many routes in the area are considered low difficulty, this specific circular trail involves moderate sections due to elevation changes, especially when ascending to the San Torcaz peak and the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda. It's best suited for hikers who are comfortable with moderate slopes and uneven, sometimes rocky, terrain.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended, while winter might present colder conditions and potentially some snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, it's always a good practice to keep them on a leash, especially around livestock, wildlife, and other hikers, and to clean up after them. Be mindful of any specific signage around the sanctuary grounds.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Buezo?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting from the population of Buezo, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots that do not obstruct local traffic or access for residents.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or visit the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda. Access to the natural areas and the sanctuary is generally free.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or natural features will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several significant points of interest. These include the historic Sanctuary of Doña Casilda, dramatically situated on a limestone crag. You'll also pass by the ancient natural springs of Pozo Blanco and Pozo Negro, both with local legends. The route also takes you to the Alto de la Varga and San Torcaz summits, offering panoramic views of the Bureba region.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery on this hike?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers excellent panoramic views. From elevated points like the San Torcaz peak and the Alto de la Varga, you'll get expansive vistas of the entire Bureba region. The approach to the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda also provides picturesque views of the sanctuary itself.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route follow parts of the Camino de Rojas, an ancient pathway that adds to the historical context of your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities available in Buezo or near the trail?
faqs.answer: Buezo is a small village, so amenities might be limited. It's advisable to bring your own water and snacks. For more extensive facilities like cafes or restaurants, you might need to visit larger nearby towns such as Briviesca or Rojas de Bureba before or after your hike.
